meta.name: Cold War - Cuban Missile Crisis
meta.author: Historia
meta.description: October 1962. The world stands on the brink of nuclear war. Soviet missiles in Cuba threaten the United States. Tensions are at an all-time high.

armies.name: Army of Korea, Democratic People's Republic of

nodes.name: Cuban Missile Crisis
nodes.description: Soviet nuclear missiles discovered in Cuba, triggering a 13-day standoff that brings the world to the brink of nuclear war.
nodes.narrativePrompt: Describe the Cuban Missile Crisis and the tense 13-day standoff between the US and USSR.

nodes.name: Sino-Soviet Split
nodes.description: Relations between China and the USSR deteriorate, fracturing the communist bloc.
nodes.narrativePrompt: Describe the growing rift between China and the Soviet Union.

nodes.name: Vietnam War Escalation
nodes.description: Following the Gulf of Tonkin incident, the US commits ground troops to Vietnam.
nodes.narrativePrompt: Describe the escalation of the Vietnam War following the Gulf of Tonkin incident.

nodes.name: African Decolonization Wave
nodes.description: A wave of independence sweeps across Africa as European colonial empires retreat.
nodes.narrativePrompt: Describe the wave of African independence as colonial empires retreat from the continent.

nodes.name: Moon Landing
nodes.description: The United States lands a man on the moon, winning the Space Race and boosting American prestige worldwide.
nodes.narrativePrompt: Describe the Apollo 11 Moon landing and its impact on the Cold War.

nodes.name: Détente
nodes.description: A period of relaxed tensions between the US and USSR. Arms limitation treaties are signed.
nodes.narrativePrompt: Describe the period of Détente and the signing of SALT treaties.

nodes.name: Soviet-Afghan War
nodes.description: The USSR invades Afghanistan, ending Détente and beginning a decade-long quagmire.
nodes.narrativePrompt: Describe the Soviet invasion of Afghanistan and its consequences for superpower relations.

nodes.name: Fall of the Berlin Wall
nodes.description: The Berlin Wall falls as East Germans flood through to the West. Germany begins reunification.

preventionConditions.description: East Germany maintains stability through reforms

nodes.narrativePrompt: Describe the fall of the Berlin Wall and the emotional reunification of Germany.

nodes.name: Dissolution of the USSR
nodes.description: The Soviet Union collapses. Russia and 14 other republics emerge as independent states.
nodes.narrativePrompt: Describe the dissolution of the Soviet Union — the end of the Cold War and the birth of the Russian Federation.

narrative.introduction: CLASSIFIED - TOP SECRET. October 1962. Intelligence confirms the deployment of Soviet nuclear missiles in Cuba, just 90 miles from the American mainland. The clock is ticking.
